claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sigma(n) >= n + 1) and ((sigma(n) == n + 1) == is_prime(n))

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes p and q, then sigma(n) >= n + p + q + 1.
predicate: (not (is_prime(n) or len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p]) == 0)) or (sigma(n) >= n + [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][0] + [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][1] + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if sigma(n) = n + 2, then n is the square of a prime.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sigma(n) != n + 2) or (any(p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is even, then sigma(n) >= n + n/2 + 1.
For all n > 1, if n is even and not divisible by 3, then sigma(n) >= n + n/2 + 2.
For all n > 1, if n is a power of 2, then sigma(n) = 2*n - 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not (n & (n-1) == 0 and n != 0)) or (sigma(n) == 2*n - 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) = n/2.
For all n > 1, if n is a power of 2, then sigma(n) + phi(n) == (5*n)/2 - 1.
For all n > 1, if n is a perfect number, then sigma(n) = 2*n.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sigma(n) != 2*n) or (sum(d for d in range(1, n) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is an even perfect number, then n is of the form 2^(p-1)*(2^p - 1) where 2^p - 1 is prime.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (n % 2 != 0) or (sigma(n) != 2*n) or (any((2**(p-1))*(2**p - 1) == n and is_prime(2**p - 1) for p in range(2, int(math.log(n, 2))+2)))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is an even perfect number, then n is congruent to 1 modulo 9.
For all n > 1, if n is a product of three distinct primes, then sigma(n) >= n + p + q + r + p*q + p*r + q*r + 1 where p, q, r are the prime factors.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) != 3) or (sigma(n) >= n + sum([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) + sum([p*q for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) for q in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % q == 0 and is_prime(q) and is_prime(n // q) and p != q]) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a square of a prime, then sigma(n) = n + sqrt(n) + 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1))) or (sigma(n) == n + int(n**0.5) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a square of a prime, then phi(n) = n - sqrt(n).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1))) or (phi(n) == n - int(n**0.5))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a square of a prime, then sigma(n) + phi(n) = 2*n + 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1))) or (sigma(n) + phi(n) == 2*n + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a semiprime (product of two primes, not necessarily distinct), then sigma(n) >= n + p + q + 1 where p, q are the prime factors.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) != 2) or (sigma(n) >= n + sum([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a semiprime with distinct prime factors p and q, then sigma(n) - phi(n) = p + q.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p]) != 2) or (sigma(n) - phi(n) == sum([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p]))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a cube of a prime, then sigma(n) = n + n^(2/3) + n^(1/3) + 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**(1/3))+1))) or (sigma(n) == n + int(n**(2/3)) + int(n**(1/3)) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a cube of a prime, then phi(n) = n - n^(2/3).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**(1/3))+1))) or (phi(n) == n - int(n**(2/3)))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a cube of a prime, then sigma(n) + phi(n) = 2*n + n^(1/3) + 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p*p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**(1/3))+1))) or (sigma(n) + phi(n) == 2*n + int(n**(1/3)) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a product of four distinct primes, then sigma(n) >= n + p + q + r + s + p*q + p*r + p*s + q*r + q*s + r*s + p*q*r + p*q*s + p*r*s + q*r*s + 1 where p, q, r, s are the prime factors.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) != 4) or (sigma(n) >= n + sum([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p)]) + sum([p*q for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) for q in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % q == 0 and is_prime(q) and is_prime(n // q) and p != q]) + sum([p*q*r for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) for q in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % q == 0 and is_prime(q) and is_prime(n // q) and p != q for r in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % r == 0 and is_prime(r) and is_prime(n // r) and r != p and r != q]) + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then sigma(n) = (p^(k+1) - 1)/(p - 1) where k is the exponent.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p**k == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) for k in range(1, int(math.log(n, p))+2))) or (sigma(n) == (p**(k+1) - 1)//(p - 1) where p is [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)][0] and k is [k for k in range(1, int(math.log(n, p))+2) if p**k == n][0])

claim: For all n > 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then phi(n) = n - n/p.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(p**k == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) for k in range(1, int(math.log(n, p))+2))) or (phi(n) == n - n//[p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)][0])
